highlights: Conan was caught holding a nail clipper mid-interview and Tanner somehow found this iconic
Tanner described airbag backpacks and breathing tubes for avalanche survival in genuine detail
'There's a steam cloud that follows me wherever I go when I'm skiing' — Conan, about himself
Avalanche Terrain.fanQuestion: Have you ever had to be rescued by ski patrol? If not, what do you think your injury would be?
Avalanche Terrain.conanResponse: Conan said he's never needed rescue but described himself as a trail of steam and chaos on skis, then imagined his injury would be something embarrassing and entirely self-inflicted.

highlights: Logan found Conan while presumably searching for something entirely unrelated
Conan absolutely refused to be drawn into any urological discussion about himself personally
Matt Gourley made it so much worse by trying to help
Blueberry Jam.fanQuestion: How many times a night do you get up to use the bathroom?
Blueberry Jam.conanResponse: Conan flatly refused to discuss his own bathroom schedule and redirected the question back to Logan with increasing desperation while Matt Gourley fanned the flames.

highlights: Conan asks fan: 'How do you handle high-maintenance guests?'
Hannah described the soap-opera level drama that unfolds aboard superyachts when crew start dating each other
Conan designed his ideal yacht name: 'HMS Dump Truck'
Revealed she once rode out a hurricane while trapped aboard because someone has to watch the boat
Rusty found the Conan podcast while searching for something entirely else online — Conan found this hilarious and touching
Has had spinal muscular atrophy his whole life and traveled to the US since age 13 seeking treatment
Told Conan that the podcast 'made the pandemic worth pushing through' — a genuinely moving moment
Grand Theft Rickshaw.fanQuestion: If I were to come to Mumbai and we hung out together, what would you want to do with me?
Grand Theft Rickshaw.conanResponse: Conan joked he'd push Rusty through the streets of Mumbai in a wheelchair as a crowd-drawing attraction, and offered to be a far better wingman than Rusty's own father.

highlights: James runs what may genuinely be one of the last DVD rental stores in the world
He also has two cats named MJ and Taonga (Māori name) inherited from neighbors
His writing aspirations include a film role specifically written for Conan
The Last DVD Store.fanQuestion: I'm writing a film role specifically for you — can you think of what you could play off the top of your head?
The Last DVD Store.conanResponse: Conan proposed playing a confused Irishman who accidentally sailed to New Zealand instead of New York and took up hitman work around Christchurch.

highlights: Charles works at a candy factory — the episode practically writes itself
Conan expressed genuine grief that the candy factory isn't better known
Together they designed candy concepts that would definitely not sell
Mara has multiple taxidermied hamsters that her cats killed — she arranged this deliberately
Conan challenged grandmothers of the world to a fight after discussing Viennese escalator etiquette
The Austrian national slogan, per Mara: 'We hate you as much as you hate us'
I'm a Very Normal Person with Very Norma.conanResponse: After Mara warned him about aggressive grandmothers who beat people with walking sticks for using the wrong escalator side, Conan dramatically challenged all grandmothers worldwide to a fight.

highlights: Sam is a devoted cryptid researcher and true believer in the Mothman of Point Pleasant, West Virginia
Conan was dragged deeper and deeper into the Mothman mythos despite his best efforts
The conversation somehow ended with Conan half-convinced the Mothman is real and watching him specifically
The Mothman Cometh.fanQuestion: Do you ever wish they still made the O'Taco at Taco Bell?
The Mothman Cometh.conanResponse: Conan claimed he personally designed an Irish-themed taco with corned beef, potatoes, and Guinness that was the biggest seller at a Dublin Taco Bell, and couldn't understand why it was never rolled out nationwide.

highlights: Rahil is a gastroenterology fellow whose daily job involves things you don't want described at dinner
Conan and Rahil bonded over the absurdity of in-law negotiations and the concept of 'face'
Rahil's ring-shopping strategy was both medically inventive and deeply inadvisable
One Ring To Rule Them All.fanQuestion: I'm about to propose to my girlfriend, but her parents are jewelers — how would you navigate the conflict of interest when you have no negotiating power?
One Ring To Rule Them All.conanResponse: Conan suggested simply buying from the parents since they'd offer a fair deal, then launched into an elaborate bit about Rahil swallowing the ring to bypass the in-laws entirely.

highlights: Eugenia has a Spanish mother and an Irish-American fiancé named Sean, and the cultural collision is ongoing
Conan related deeply to Sean's uptight Irish inability to express affection in public
The concept of a Seville wedding with Irish guests was explored in unnecessarily specific detail
Suspiciously Healthy.fanQuestion: My Irish-American fiancé isn't comfortable with public displays of affection — how do I help him open up?
Suspiciously Healthy.conanResponse: Conan, relating as a fellow uptight Irish person, offered to personally coach Sean on being 'a little more of a Latin lover' and volunteered to manage their Seville wedding by keeping the Irish guests in line.

highlights: Nikhil is a video creator and talk show host from Bangalore with a very specific vision for his show
Conan was immediately intrigued by the idea of a show where he visits fans at home — which is basically Must Go
Nikhil's interview style was confident enough that Conan offered to appear as a guest on his pilot
Rascally Nincompoop.fanQuestion: Could I help you develop a talk show concept where you visit people at their own homes?
Rascally Nincompoop.conanResponse: Conan enthusiastically agreed to collaborate, offering to serve as both guest and coach for Nikhil's pilot episode, noting the idea was suspiciously close to Must Go.

highlights: Mathew comes from a multi-generational circus family — clowning is genuinely in his blood
Conan was fascinated by the internal politics and hierarchy of a real working circus
Mathew's son has already been marked for the circus life, whether he likes it or not

Mathew told Conan: 'Your playful approach to comedy has inspired me as a clown and affected my son and me deeply' — Conan admitted his own son barely knows his real name because they're always doing bits together
DeVaughn competed on the Canadian national bobsled team on the World Cup circuit
He used to sneak-watch Conan on a tiny 15-inch TV as a kid in Calgary — Conan found this incredibly touching
The episode ended with Conan promising to put his face on DeVaughn's bobsled
Canada O'Brien.conanResponse: Conan expressed genuine warmth about loyal Canadian fans who backed him early, then promised to sponsor DeVaughn's bobsled and brand it 'Canada O'Brien.'

highlights: Vibhuti is a highly competitive badminton player living with her parents in New Delhi with her UN-employed husband Casey
Her nemesis Tolika is a recurring villain in Vibhuti's badminton journey
Conan is now personally invested in Tolika's defeat
Born to Be Badminton.fanQuestion: Could you help me improve my badminton skills so we could team up and I could finally claim my spot as the best player?
Born to Be Badminton.conanResponse: Conan promised to help despite never playing badminton, claiming his height and unusual appearance would confuse opponents, and vowed they would defeat her rival Tolika who was almost certainly 'juicing.'

highlights: Glenn works in breakfast radio in Auckland and takes the job of being Conan-adjacent very seriously

Glenn explained he called in because being on the show made him only two degrees of separation from Harrison Ford and Jeff Goldblum — Conan corrected the math: knowing Conan directly makes it one degree
Harrison Ford emerged as a surprising focal point of the celebrity proximity discussion
Anirudh (Ani) has been watching Conan since age 15 and once sent an angry diversity complaint email to Team Coco
The email began 'Hey, you. Yeah, you.' — Conan found the approach both alarming and charming
Anirudh's girlfriend Irene was brought into the conversation and Conan offered relationship counsel
It's A Small World In Here.conanResponse: Conan approved of Anirudh after learning about his cheeky diversity email and offered to personally vouch for him with Irene's parents if needed.

highlights: Mustafa is half Filipino, half Arabic, quit a 10-year Dubai career to pursue action stardom in the Philippines
His martial arts training and charisma convinced Conan he could genuinely be a star
Conan promised to get in shape specifically to meet Mustafa
Oatmeal Milk.conanResponse: Conan expressed full belief in Mustafa's action star destiny, promised to get in amazing shape before they meet, and offered to financially support Mustafa's film career.

highlights: Conan surprised Jarle with an unannounced visit to his home — Jarle was barely holding it together
Jarle gave Conan a guided tour of his neighborhood and a crash course in Norwegian life
Jarle's family turned out to be equally excited to meet Conan, which somehow surprised everyone
Kai walked Conan through Bergen's historic Bryggen wharf and local fish market
Conan tried fresh Norwegian fish and developed strong opinions about Scandinavian seafood
Kai proved a patient and knowledgeable guide who somehow matched Conan's energy throughout
Sebastian's Buenos Aires studio was full of artwork he'd created inspired directly by Conan
Conan got a private tour of some of the most striking street art in the city
Sebastian could not quite believe that Conan O'Brien had actually flown to Buenos Aires to meet him
Matias took Conan to a proper Argentine parrilla for the full Buenos Aires steakhouse experience
Conan's attempts at Argentine Spanish were enthusiastically attempted and enthusiastically corrected
Matias demonstrated that Argentine fan hospitality is genuinely second to none
Cami became the first female fan to be visited by Conan on 'Must Go' — an honor she wore proudly
Conan and Cami explored a classic Buenos Aires café and discussed everyday life in Argentina
Cami's reaction upon seeing Conan at her door was genuinely one for the highlight reel
Anna guided Conan through a Bangkok street food market and gently tested his adventurousness
Conan encountered several dishes he had previously had absolutely no concept of
Anna was a remarkably patient and fun guide, even when Conan could not pronounce anything
Whitney showed Conan a side of Thailand that goes almost entirely unnoticed by tourists
Conan's attempts to blend in with locals were exactly as successful as you would expect
Whitney was equally delighted and mortified by Conan's presence in her neighborhood
Mohammed showed Conan that Ireland has far more cultural diversity than most visitors expect
Conan got a proper tour of the local pub and Mohammed's regulars were unexpectedly starstruck
Mohammed fielded a lot of questions about exactly how he ended up in the Irish countryside
Reilly builds livable homes out of repurposed shipping containers in remote Hokitika, New Zealand
Conan was visibly impressed by the finished homes and deeply skeptical about the building process
Reilly's laid-back attitude about turning giant metal boxes into beautiful homes was infectious
Katherina plays in a heavy metal band in Austria — which surprised Conan more than it probably should
Conan got a brief but alarming lesson in the Austrian heavy metal scene
Katherina's guitar skills were demonstrated live and Conan was appropriately humbled
Muntasser left Iraq and built an entirely new life in Vienna, and has strong feelings about walnut trees
Conan and Muntasser discussed what it feels like to be a cultural outsider who deeply loves your adopted country
Muntasser's warmth and deadpan humor made this one of the most moving Must Go visits
Penny had just gotten engaged when Conan showed up — she was still visibly buzzing with excitement
Conan offered generous amounts of unsolicited wedding advice, which Penny received with admirable patience
Penny's fiancé had absolutely no idea that Conan O'Brien was about to show up at their house
Noah works at a company that scientifically sizes men for condoms — a topic Conan had many questions about
The measurement process turned out to involve considerably more dignity than Conan had assumed
Conan navigated the entire conversation without once making it about himself, which is a personal best
We Have A Condom For That.fanQuestion: Do you think knowing your correct condom size would change your life?
We Have A Condom For That.conanResponse: Conan said yes, absolutely, and expressed deep personal regret that this technology wasn't available when he was younger, before catching himself and changing the subject.

highlights: Conan asks fan: 'What do you think you'd do if you caught a hotel guest doing something truly outrageous?'
Trisha runs a small hotel in the Philippines and moonlights as its in-house detective
The hotel detective role involves solving disputes, missing property cases, and a surprising amount of drama
Conan and Trisha bonded over their shared belief that most people are up to something
Josh co-founded a company selling direct-to-consumer caskets and urns — cutting out the funeral home markup
The casket industry's pricing turned out to be even more eyebrow-raising than Conan had expected
Josh believes that shopping for your own casket is actually an act of love for your family
Putting The Fun Back In Funeral.fanQuestion: Have you given any thought to what you want for your own funeral?
Putting The Fun Back In Funeral.conanResponse: Conan said he wants something 'theatrical and confusing,' possibly involving his body being launched from a catapult while an orchestra plays, to ensure everyone is too bewildered to grieve.

highlights: Ankur built a detailed Excel spreadsheet to track and optimize happiness in his marriage
Conan found the existence of this spreadsheet both deeply unsettling and secretly impressive
The metrics Ankur uses to measure marital satisfaction turned out to be surprisingly thoughtful
A Strong Dose.fanQuestion: On a scale of 1 to 10, how happy are you with your marriage right now, and what would make it an 11?
A Strong Dose.conanResponse: Conan gave himself a solid 8 and said the path to 11 involved his wife Liza watching less true crime and him being allowed to stay up past 11pm on weekends.

highlights: Yasmina walked Conan through the unwritten rules of haggling in the Marrakesh souks
Conan discovered he would be a catastrophically bad haggler and there is no helping him
The discussion of finger foods in Moroccan culture somehow became one of the highlights of the year
The Tallest Thing in Marrakesh.fanQuestion: Do you think you could survive a full day in a Moroccan souk without getting ripped off?
The Tallest Thing in Marrakesh.conanResponse: Conan admitted he would be destroyed within minutes, confidently paying ten times the asking price for everything while thanking the merchant profusely.

highlights: Stelios explained the Greek philosophy of relaxation — which is essentially an entire way of life
Conan admitted that he has no ability to chill and asked Greece for help
The concept of 'volta' — the Greek evening stroll — genuinely moved Conan in a way he wasn't prepared for
Hot Athens.fanQuestion: What's your most stressful habit, and do you think you could ever truly relax?
Hot Athens.conanResponse: Conan said his most stressful habit is preparing for everything that could possibly go wrong, then worrying about things that couldn't, and admitted that Greece was the first place to almost break through his anxiety.

highlights: Margaret designs theme parks and her current project is Haw Par Villa — Singapore's most surreal attraction
Haw Par Villa features graphic dioramas depicting the consequences of sinful behavior, and it is exactly what it sounds like
Conan and Margaret discussed the surprisingly fine line between horrifying and educational
Cocoland.fanQuestion: What would your own personal theme park look like?
Cocoland.conanResponse: Conan designed a theme park entirely dedicated to his own anxieties, where every ride ends ambiguously and the gift shop sells only things that are slightly wrong.

highlights: Lucy is a jazz singer who specializes in scat — the wordless vocal improvisation technique
Conan attempted scat and the results were enthusiastically received and never spoken of again
Lucy explained that scat is essentially having a conversation with the band using only sounds
The Jazz Singer.fanQuestion: Do you have any secret musical talents, or is it all just being tall and pale?
The Jazz Singer.conanResponse: Conan claimed to have a deeply suppressed gift for jazz scat that had been crushed by his years of television, then demonstrated something that Lucy generously described as 'a start.'

highlights: Joey works to preserve rare and historically significant home movies, including some from famous families
The film collections he has worked with include footage that no living person has seen in decades
Conan was deeply invested in which celebrity home movies might exist and whether he could see them
12 Clomp Program.fanQuestion: If you could preserve one moment from your own life on film forever, what would it be?
12 Clomp Program.conanResponse: Conan said he'd preserve a quiet dinner with his family where nothing notable happened, which he claimed would be the most subversive thing he could possibly leave behind.

highlights: Micah works in advertising design — specifically making fast food look irresistible in photos
Conan became surprisingly invested in designing his own signature fast food combo
The episode ended with a fully branded 'Conan Slab-a-Roo' meal concept and a demand for royalties
The Conan Slabaroo.fanQuestion: If Conan O'Brien had a custom drink or combo, what would it be?
The Conan Slabaroo.conanResponse: Conan said his combo had to be ham-based to honor his Irish heritage, eventually naming it 'The Conan Slab-a-Roo' — a large ham sandwich with fries and a drink — and insisted he should be paid hundreds of dollars for lending his name to any such product.

highlights: Trisha covers real cold cases for a living — Conan found this simultaneously terrifying and cool
She has personally interviewed serial killers in prison as part of her investigative work
Conan walked away from this one with genuine respect and a slight wariness of Trisha
Bit@#es Be Hitchin'.fanQuestion: What advice would you give me the next time I go in and interview a serial killer?
Bit@#es Be Hitchin'.conanResponse: Conan advised finding common ground immediately — even comparing his own experience of 'killing' audiences through bad comedy to the killer's crimes — to build rapport that might eventually yield a confession.

highlights: Jon has webbed toes, which he apparently leads with as a fun fact in every social situation
He is also starting his own juice bar, which turned out to be slightly less interesting than the webbed toes
Conan became Jüs'd's unofficial marketing consultant by the end of the episode
Jüs'd.fanQuestion: People always ask 'are you a fast swimmer?' because of my webbed feet — can you help me come up with better responses?
Jüs'd.conanResponse: Conan suggested Jon respond by claiming to be an incredible swimmer with a knowing look and leaving it there, or alternatively act deeply offended and walk out of the room before returning as if nothing happened.

highlights: Koko is a Sound Shaman who creates immersive audio environments for healing and meditation
Conan spent most of the episode trying to understand exactly what a Sound Shaman does
The Mr. Burns question somehow derailed the whole conversation in the best possible way
The Bear and the Bath.fanQuestion: Have you ever considered doing a live-action Mr. Burns Broadway play?
The Bear and the Bath.conanResponse: Conan explained he doesn't own the rights — 'the Simpsons develops something like that, they have to reach out to you' — and joked that as an Irishman, 'Fiddler on the Roof is where I belong' anyway.

highlights: Conan asks fan: 'Have you ever been genuinely scared by a polar bear, or do you just become numb to it?'
Mike works for a conservation authority that monitors polar bears in the Canadian wilderness
The polar bear monitoring process involves considerably more bureaucracy than most people would imagine
Conan and Mike bonded over the absurdity of having to file paperwork about polar bear encounters

Fanhausen Revisited.fanQuestion: What do you think about having a wrestling persona inspired entirely by you?
highlights: Donovan created 'Danhausen' — a minor league wrestling character directly inspired by Conan — years before the WWE came calling
The episode originally aired April 15, 2021, when Donovan was still in the independent circuit; it was re-aired in 2026 to mark his WWE debut
Donovan was the very first fan to ever call into Conan O'Brien Needs a Fan, making him a founding piece of the show's history
Conan asks fan: 'What has it been like to be the very first fan to ever call in to Conan O'Brien Needs a Fan?'
Leigh-Anne holds the distinction of being the very first fan to ever call in to Conan O'Brien Needs a Fan — she and Donovan were the two guests on the show's very first episode
A Mechanical Engineer from Toronto, Leigh-Anne was chosen for the inaugural episode alongside Donovan (now-famous wrestler Danhausen)
Her call-in helped launch what became one of the most beloved recurring podcast segments in comedy
Conan asks fan: 'What inspired you to start FreedomReads and bring libraries to prison cellblocks?'
Reginald turned a past mistake into purpose — founding FreedomReads.org, a nonprofit that places fully-stocked libraries directly inside prison cellblocks
FreedomReads has transformed the experience of incarceration for thousands of people by making books accessible in the places they are needed most
Reginald's story is one of the most powerful on the show — a fan who called in not just to talk to Conan, but to spread the word about a cause that changes lives
Phillip hunts for lost relics around Colonial Williamsburg — and has occasionally unearthed live hand grenades along the way
Conan asks fan: 'What is the strangest or most dangerous thing you have ever found while relic hunting?'
His golden rule: always fill in your holes after a search, which is apparently less obvious than it sounds
Chris performs as luchador persona 'The Prophet,' blending wrestling showmanship with real athleticism.
Conan and Chris explore the theatrical spectacle of lucha libre and its over-the-top character work.
Chris explains the dedication required to live the luchador lifestyle both inside and outside the ring.
Sandra runs a perfume shop in Lakewood, CA and walks Conan through creating custom scents.
Conan and Sandra discuss what it takes for a TikTok to go viral in the fragrance space.
Sandra breaks down the art of matching a scent to a customer's personality and preferences.
Derek, a Silicon Valley tech professional, gives Conan an insider look at life in the Bay Area.
Conan and Derek riff on the quirks and culture of working in the tech industry.
Derek shares his hobbies and interests that set him apart from the typical Silicon Valley stereotype.
Arjun from Kerala studies Russian — an unusual language choice that immediately intrigues Conan.
Conan and Arjun discuss Kerala's rich history as a spice trading hub and cultural crossroads.
Arjun considers whether Conan could actually serve as his wingman in his search for a partner.
Yassir from Casablanca schools Conan on the unique menu items at Moroccan McDonald's locations.
Conan and Yassir debate what makes Casablanca's local food culture distinct and worth celebrating.
Yassir shares what life in Morocco's biggest city is actually like for a young local expert.
Sid, a cheese innovation expert in Budapest, explains the cutting edge of modern cheese development.
Conan probes Sid on what 'cheese innovation' actually means and which experiments have gone wrong.
The episode features Matt Gourley stepping in as host while Conan takes a back seat.
Asya recounts the chicken sandwich experience that triggered an unexpected culinary revelation.
Conan and Asya dig into how a single meal can completely reshape someone's relationship with food.
Asya's story takes surprising turns as she unpacks what the sandwich actually led her to discover.
Tasha, a psychotherapist in Zagreb, gives Conan an inside look at mental health culture in Croatia.
Conan and Tasha discuss what brings people to therapy in Zagreb and how attitudes differ from the US.
Tasha explains what the episode title's 'Zagrebian attitude' actually means to locals.
Nurzhamal from Kazakhstan quizzes Conan on how much he actually knows about her home country.
Conan stumbles through his limited Kazakhstan knowledge, leading to good-natured corrections from Nurzhamal.
Nurzhamal shares what Kazakhstani cuisine — including horse meat — is really like for locals.
Lucas plays professional basketball in the Netherlands and explains how European leagues differ from the NBA.
Conan and Lucas discuss the reality of being an American pro athlete living and competing abroad.
Lucas breaks down the Dutch basketball scene and what it takes to stand out on a European roster.
Mike co-founded The Forest Farmers in Lake Placid and walks Conan through the many uses of birch sap.
The episode includes a live Chill Chums syrup taste test that puts Conan's palate to the test.
Mike explains why birch sap is a surprisingly versatile and underappreciated ingredient.
Phil coordinates adventure-based team building, and Conan tries to understand why anyone would enjoy it.
Phil explains how activities like rock climbing and rope courses actually foster workplace collaboration.
Conan and Phil debate whether corporate team building ever genuinely works or is just awkward.
Maaike stands 6 feet tall and shares what the international high fashion modeling world is really like.
Conan and Maaike discuss the physical and logistical realities of modeling runways in Amsterdam and beyond.
Maaike gives Conan an unfiltered look at the challenges and perks of being tall in the fashion industry.
Geography professor Stephanie explains why Richmond, VA is not her favorite place — and doesn't hold back.
Conan and Stephanie discuss what people consistently get wrong about the field of geography.
Stephanie connects her climate change research to why geographic literacy actually matters.
Max works as a meteorologist in Fargo, ND, where the weather is both his job and his daily nemesis.
Conan and Max explore what it's like to forecast weather in one of America's most extreme climates.
Max explains the particular challenges and absurdities of telling Fargo residents what they already know.
Glassblower Dan from Vacaville, CA describes his most intricate and elaborate glass creations.
Conan and Dan discuss how Conan allegedly pulls his weight around the household — a claim Conan defends.
Dan explains the skill and danger involved in working with molten glass as an art form.
Aadithya (Aadu) works at the University of Washington School of Oceanography and describes his research.
Aadu assigns specific birds to Conan, Matt, and Sona — and explains his ornithological reasoning.
Conan and Aadu discuss Aadu's upcoming wedding and how his life straddles science and celebration.
Civil War historian Cooper explains how to weave Civil War discourse naturally into a first date conversation.
Conan and Cooper debate how Conan would have actually fared as a soldier on the Civil War battlefield.
Cooper uses a PowerPoint-style breakdown to make Civil War history accessible and surprisingly funny.
Rianne from Taguig, Philippines is vertically challenged and describes the daily struggles that come with it.
Conan and Rianne bond over the indignities of navigating a world not built for shorter people.
Rianne's blunt, unfiltered takes on crowd navigation give the episode its memorable title.
Yousra from Rabat makes the case that Moroccan street food is among the highest quality in the world.
Conan and Yousra explore Rabat's club scene and best spa options, with Yousra as expert guide.
Yousra explains the title — why asking directions in Rabat is a uniquely unhelpful experience.
Catalonian twins Alba and Berta teach Conan how to be more chill and spontaneous when invited to events.
Conan admits he struggles with impromptu social situations, and the twins attempt to coach him.
Alba and Berta explain the relaxed Catalonian approach to social life and why Conan needs it.
Javier works as a tourism information agent near Madrid and shares the strangest requests tourists make.
Conan and Javier compare notes on the most absurd things visitors ask about Spanish culture and history.
Javier explains how his job as a handyman and tourist guide puts him at the intersection of two worlds.
Vasco from Portugal walks Conan through the region's rich history and why its borders have stayed stable.
Conan and Vasco debate Portuguese delicacies, with Vasco steering Conan toward the best options.
Vasco floats the idea of Conan as a surfing wingman, a role Conan seems entirely unqualified for.
Jules and wife Maria join from Miami; Jules has ALS and has used AI technology to preserve his voice.
Conan and Jules discuss the daily challenges and unexpected inspirations of living with ALS.
Jules and Maria share how their podcast The Couple Shift tells their story and connects with others.
Etta works as an LGBTQ+ sober living counselor and describes what her clients navigate day to day.
Conan and Etta explore how sober living intersects specifically with LGBTQ+ community challenges.
Etta explains what drew her to this work and what keeps her committed to it.
Julia explains how looms work and name-drops big-name celebrity weavers Conan has never heard of.
Conan learns Julia engineers biomedical textiles — actual human-body-grade fabrics — from her Ithaca studio.
The conversation spirals into whether weaving is a competitive sport and who the LeBron James of looms is.
Pam explains the wildest calls she gets at Poison Control, including panicked parents and accidental ingestions.
Conan and Pam discuss how to handle being too high at Disneyland — a surprisingly common Poison Control inquiry.
The episode title is explained: certain foods and substances produce a colorful result that alarms callers.
Ben from Seattle builds and plays massive pipe organs, including the instrument at Kraken NHL games.
Conan marvels at how a single pipe organ can fill an entire arena with sound during hockey games.
Ben details the engineering complexity of pipe organ construction — thousands of pipes, each hand-tuned.
Lani explains what a tour manager actually does — logistics, babysitting artists, and preventing disasters.
Conan asks Lani how he would calm a frightened child who found him terrifying, leading to a creative answer.
The episode title references Lani's name featuring a macron — a long vowel marker unfamiliar to most Americans.
Michael Jay is a retired clown from Kitty Hawk who now works as a death doula — two very different callings.
Conan and Michael discuss what kind of classic clown Conan would be, with Michael offering a detailed diagnosis.
Michael explains 'benevolent clowning' — spreading joy rather than terror — and how clown culture has evolved.
Brad, a real miner from Manitoulin Island, breaks down efficient mine design and underground safety systems.
Conan asks Brad how deep he could bury himself — Brad provides a disturbingly precise and practical answer.
Brad assesses which specific mining job Conan would be best suited for, concluding it's a supervisory role.
Anthony works in cybersecurity and walks Conan through how hackers exploit everyday digital vulnerabilities.
The episode covers the hidden threats lurking in common online behavior most people never think about.
Conan and Anthony explore what a cyberattack on a podcast would actually look like and who the target would be.
Brie, a Utah mother of six, describes her post-Mormon journey discovering coffee, alcohol, and freedom.
Conan learns Brie owns a bridal shop — and proceeds to design his own bridal gown in enthusiastic detail.
Brie explains raising six sons in Utah while quietly drinking rum and Cokes in the bathroom during church events.
Brandon investigates paranormal occurrences in Georgetown, Ontario and shares his scariest destinations.
Conan and Brandon analyze what Conan's unique haunting style would look like if he were a ghost.
Brandon describes the strangest evidence he's collected and how skeptics respond when confronted with footage.
Janis, a Miami sex therapist, shares professional tips for bringing more fun and connection into the bedroom.
Conan's team is assigned roles in a hypothetical sex therapy class, with Conan choosing his own subject matter.
Janis explains what the most common issues her clients bring to therapy are, surprising Conan with the answers.
Trey runs escape rooms in Los Angeles and explains how Game Masters watch and subtly guide trapped players.
Conan pitches his plan to transition from podcasting into serious dramatic acting, with Trey's reluctant feedback.
Trey describes the most chaotic escape room group he's ever monitored and how close they came to destroying everything.
Liz, a Providence children's librarian, explains the increasingly chaotic demands of modern library programming.
Conan is assigned a role in a fairy tale world by Liz — and the result is not a heroic one.
Liz discusses how children's libraries have changed and why her job is equal parts storytelling and crowd control.
Steven from Silver City is both an astrologer and a jeweler — and explains how the two disciplines intersect.
Conan is told which stone he should wear based on his birth chart, and he's skeptical but oddly invested.
Steven and Conan discuss whether Conan's belief in the power of objects is secretly a form of astrology.
Jonah sells used cars in Tupelo, MS and shares the psychological tricks that make a reluctant buyer say yes.
Conan develops his own universal sales pitch on the fly, which Jonah critiques with surprising professionalism.
The conversation pivots to Jonah's tattoos and how they affect customer trust on the used car lot.
Tom from Dublin makes the case that Bram Stoker — born in Dublin — should be reclaimed as an Irish icon.
Conan pitches wild ideas for the annual Bram Stoker festival, including events that horrify Tom slightly.
Tom explains how Stoker's Irish identity has been overshadowed by Dracula's Transylvanian setting.
Sean, a Halifax naturalist, recounts the strangest and most alarming things he's found while diving.
Sean describes his long-running rivalry with a local snapping turtle that refuses to yield territory.
Conan and Sean bond over the idea that nature is both magnificent and deeply, personally inconvenient.
Isabel from West Virginia is building a fully self-sustaining homestead and explains what that actually requires.
Conan is asked how he would fill his time if forced entirely off the grid — his answer is panicked and detailed.
Isabel breaks down the hardest skills to learn on a homestead: food preservation, animal husbandry, and water.
Chris, a Chico paramedic, shares the most surreal calls he's responded to and how he stays calm.
Chris explains why he adamantly refuses to dress up for Halloween despite being surrounded by costumes.
Conan and Chris discuss the mental toll of paramedicine and the dark humor that helps first responders cope.
Bill's dog Hondo is a German Shepherd approaching retirement age — and the conversation centers on dog careers.
Bill also worked for a funeral home, giving him an unusually philosophical view of endings, human and canine.
Conan and Bill explore what retirement looks like for working dogs and whether Hondo has earned a pension.
Amalia is the self-described Local Personality of Thames, NZ — population small enough that everyone knows everything.
Conan fantasizes about running Thames and Amalia explains what the actual politics of a tiny town look like.
The episode explores how gossip functions as community infrastructure in small-town New Zealand.
Joy is a Columbus zookeeper who introduces Conan to hellbenders and snot otters — giant aquatic salamanders.
Conan learns that penguins are far more aggressive than they appear and Joy has the stories to prove it.
The episode title riffs on Joy's zookeeper rank and the surprisingly military structure of zoo staff hierarchies.
Sarah is an acupuncturist who explains to Conan which body points produce the most dramatic patient reactions.
The episode title comes from Sarah matter-of-factly noting that perineum needling is a standard treatment.
Conan and Sarah discuss the skepticism acupuncture faces and what the evidence actually says about its effects.
Aoife DJs weddings in Connemara and explains the art of reading a room and finding the song that breaks the ice.
Conan and Aoife discuss how she helps shy guests discover their singing voices during receptions.

Gualter is a Portuguese master tailor who explains the dying craft of bespoke suit construction.
Conan asks Gualter what kind of suit he would make for him — Gualter answers diplomatically but with precision.
The episode explores why a truly well-made suit takes weeks and why off-the-rack can never fully replace it.
Rene from the Netherlands designs custom bicycles and explains what separates an artisan bike from a mass-produced one.
Conan learns about horse-riding underwear — a garment designed for cyclists that becomes a running joke.

Kayla has encountered Conan by chance multiple times in real life, and the episode reconstructs those meetings.

The episode is a rare case where the fan's history with Conan is the subject, not her occupation.
Terri from Atlanta is a fight promoter and former undercover police officer — a combination Conan finds irresistible.
Conan and Terri run through a practice drug sting scenario, with Conan playing an enthusiastic undercover agent.
Terri explains what the psychological training for undercover work looks like and how it changes how you see people.
Greg plays bassoon in a symphony orchestra in Kildare and explains why the bassoon is the comedian of the orchestra.
Conan and Greg discuss the strange social dynamics of orchestra life — hierarchy, rivalries, and rehearsal politics.
Greg describes what it takes to master an instrument that most listeners couldn't identify by sound alone.
Henry gives tours in County Donegal and shares the strangest questions tourists ask about Irish culture.

The episode title captures a classic English understatement that Eugenia finds baffling compared to her native tongue.
Conan and Eugenia explore which English phrases are most misleading for non-native speakers learning the language.
